prevalence.trend: increasing recognition with AQP4/MOG antibody testing

burden.mortalityRate: Elevated vs MS due to relapse severity (indirect)

vulnerablePopulations: patients with delayed access to neuroimmunology care
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: International NMOSD criteria: core clinical characteristics (optic neuritis, longitudinally extensive myelitis) with AQP4-IgG positivity or supportive MRI features; differentiate from MS

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; relapse prevention depends on immunosuppression access and specialist follow-up

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: AQP4/MOG testing availability variable; often centralized
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Acute relapses: IV steroids +/- plasmapheresis; maintenance immunosuppression (rituximab/azathioprine/mycophenolate) in specialist care
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to antibody testing, plasmapheresis, and high-cost biologics outside major centers
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Specialized neuroimmunology centers exist in major cities
